reasoning: The forecasting horizon spans just four trading days, evaluating the stock price movement from Friday, March 27 to Thursday, April 2, 2026 (as markets are closed for Good Friday on April 3). Recently, TER and other semiconductor-adjacent stocks plummeted following Google's introduction of "TurboQuant", which reduces AI memory requirements and sparked a sector-wide profit-taking selloff. TER dropped from $323.35 to $295.61 over two days, although the rate of decline slowed significantly by March 27. Weekly stock movements are historically close to random walks. While the immediate momentum is negative due to the sector rotation out of memory-related hardware, sharp pullbacks on specific news often present slight mean reversion ("dead-cat bounce") as markets reassess whether the selloff was an overreaction. Furthermore, there is an inherent slight positive drift in equities. However, the short timeframe and potent downward catalyst neutralize most of the long-term upward trend. Therefore, I'm staying extremely close to 50/50, slightly favoring a bounce, but recognizing the unpredictability of short-term volatility.
